page: Amazon Web Services r6g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 64 physical cores using AWS Graviton2 processors with Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture running at 2.5 GHz, providing a 1:1 mapping of vCPUs to physical cores. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. No local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests and cached small-block memory read bandwidth, which reaches 3778.88 GB/sec. It also demonstrates high throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, single-core performance is a relative bottleneck, with weak ratings in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core metrics. Large-block uncached memory operations and multi-threaded decompression fall into the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture offers high memory density and strong multi-threaded throughput per unit of resource, making it a viable alternative to x86-based memory-optimized instances. However, workloads requiring high single-threaded performance, advanced vector extensions, or local SSD storage will face performance or architectural tradeoffs. This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as open-source databases, in-memory caches like Redis, multi-threaded software compilation, and scale-out application servers.
description: Amazon Web Services r6g.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton2 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU execution, high cached memory read bandwidth, and excellent Redis SET and static web serving throughput. Conversely, single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks are relatively weak. Lacking x86-specific instruction sets like AVX, this instance offers a cost-efficient, high-density memory profile for multi-threaded, memory-intensive applications. It is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, software compilation, and data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 64 Graviton2 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and caching performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 64 Graviton2 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring high memory density and strong multi-core throughput for caching and database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 Graviton2 cores running at 2.5 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core execution and cached memory read bandwidth
Weak single-core performance and no local storage or GPU
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and software compilation
